questions.question: What is the largest mammal in the world?
questions.explanation: The blue whale can grow up to 100 feet long and weigh up to 200 tons.

questions.question: What is a group of lions called?
questions.explanation: A group of lions is called a pride, typically consisting of related females and their cubs.

questions.question: How many hearts does an octopus have?
questions.explanation: An octopus has three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, and one pumps blood to the body.

questions.question: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: Cheetahs can reach speeds of up to 70 mph in short bursts.

questions.question: What is a baby kangaroo called?
questions.explanation: Baby kangaroos are called joeys and develop in their mother's pouch.

questions.question: Which bird is known for its ability to mimic human speech?
questions.explanation: Parrots, especially African grey parrots, are excellent mimics.

questions.question: What is the largest species of bear?
questions.explanation: Polar bears are the largest bear species, with males weighing up to 1,500 pounds.

questions.question: How many legs does a spider have?
questions.explanation: All spiders have eight legs, distinguishing them from insects which have six.

questions.question: What is the only mammal capable of true flight?
questions.explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of sustained flight.

questions.question: What is the largest species of penguin?
questions.explanation: Emperor penguins are the largest, standing up to 4 feet tall and weighing up to 100 pounds.
